name: Kief Protective Mutual Benefit Ass.
description_short: Federally insured state-chartered credit union in Bloomfield, CT serving 109 members with $685,687 in assets. Offers basic banking services including checking, savings, and loan products.
description_long:
Kief Protective Mutual Benefit Association Credit Union is a long-established financial cooperative founded in 1945, making it 81 years old at the time of this profile. It is headquartered in Bloomfield, Connecticut and operates as a federally insured state-chartered credit union under charter number 61265. The organization serves approximately 109 members with a single branch location and manages total assets of $685,687 as of September 30, 2025.

The credit union offers standard consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio includes real estate loans ($3,416), unsecured personal loans ($144,313), and other loan categories totaling $315,469 in outstanding loans. Members have $544,135 in total shares and deposits held at the institution, indicating member confidence in their operations.

Kief Protective operates as a state-chartered natural person membership credit union, meaning membership is available to individuals rather than being employer-sponsored or field-of-membership restricted. The institution maintains professional leadership with Nestor F. Palacio as CEO/Manager and Brook Jason as Chairperson. Their last regulatory audit was completed on September 1, 2024, and they are not members of the Federal Home Loan Bank system.

Financially, the credit union reported a net loss of $3,520 for the most recent reporting period, with loan income of $15,652 and investment income of $7,488 offset by $12,159 in employee compensation and $12,557 in other operating expenses. The organization maintains an equity position of $139,041 with an allowance for loan losses of $8,905, suggesting prudent credit management. For a small community credit union, this institution provides basic financial services to a stable, local membership base. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Federally insured deposits through NCUSIF provide member account protection
81 years of operational history demonstrates institutional stability and longevity
Local community-based credit union with single branch in Bloomfield, CT for personalized service
Offers ATM/debit card access for member convenience
State-chartered natural person membership allows broad community access
Maintains loan loss reserves ($8,905 allowance) indicating prudent credit practices
No FHLB membership requirements or restrictions typical of larger institutions
cons: Very small asset base ($685,687) limits product diversity and technological capabilities compared to larger institutions
Single branch location provides no geographic flexibility for members outside Bloomfield area
Recent net loss of $3,520 raises questions about operational sustainability and profitability
Only 109 members limits economies of scale and may result in higher operating costs per member
Limited information available online (no website listed) suggests minimal digital banking infrastructure
best_for: Bloomfield, CT residents seeking basic banking services from a community-focused credit union
Members prioritizing personal relationships and local decision-making over branch network size
Individuals with simple banking needs (checking, savings, basic loans) who value NCUSIF insurance protection
Community members seeking to support a long-established local financial institution
diagnosis: Kief Protective Mutual Benefit Association Credit Union is best suited for Bloomfield-area residents who value a community-based financial institution with 81 years of history and basic banking services. The primary caveat is the institution's very small size, recent operational losses, minimal online presence, and single-branch model, which may limit accessibility and digital banking capabilities for members needing modern financial technology or multi-location convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
